description: A TOCTOU (Time-of-Check-Time-of-Use) race condition in JSTypedArray::GetVariableLengthOrOutOfBounds() (src/objects/js-array-buffer.cc:451) causes integer underflow, enabling arbitrary address read/write and V8 sandbox bypass. For length-tracking TypedArrays backed by a shared resizable ArrayBuffer (GSAB), the backing store's byte_length is fetched twice from a shared atomic location: once for the out-of-bounds check and again to compute the return value. An attacker can race a worker thread to flip byte_length between the two fetches — large on fetch #1 so the bounds check passes, then small on fetch #2 — causing the subtraction (byte_length - byte_offset) to underflow and return an extremely large length. This corrupted length propagates through GetLength() into TypedArray builtins such as copyWithin(), subarray(), and others, where it is used for bounds checking and memmove operations, ultimately granting arbitrary read/write across the full 64-bit address space and bypassing the V8 sandbox.

fixes.evidence_text: Local patch generated from verified V8 commit de42e588f4f7bafc773e630f937407924528cb36 for CL 6289045.
